go:359806.title: GATE ECE 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 1
go:359806.question:
The current population of a city is $11,02,500$ . If it has been increasing at the rate of $5\%$ per annum, what was its population $2$ years ago?

  1. $9,92,500$
  2. $9,95,006$
  3. $10,00,000$
  4. $12,51,506$

go:359803.title: GATE ECE 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 2
go:359803.question:
$p$ and $q$ are positive integers and $\dfrac{p}{q}+\dfrac{q}{p}=3,$ then, $\dfrac{p^{2}}{q^{2}}+\dfrac{q^{2}}{p^{2}}=$

  1. $3$
  2. $7$
  3. $9$
  4. $11$

go:359801.title: GATE ECE 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 3
go:359801.question:
The least number of squares that must be added so that the line $P-Q$ becomes the line of symmetry is ____________

  1. $4$
  2. $3$
  3. $6$
  4. $7$

go:359799.title: GATE ECE 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 4
go:359799.question:
Nostalgia is to anticipation as ____________ is to _____________

Which one of the following options maintains a similar logical relation in the above sentence?

  1. Present, past
  2. Future, past
  3. Past, future
  4. Future, present

go:359797.title: GATE ECE 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 5
go:359797.question:
Consider the following sentences:

  1. I woke up from sleep.
  2. I woked up from sleep.
  3. I was woken up from sleep.
  4. I was wokened up from sleep.

Which of the above sentences are grammatically $\text{CORRECT}$?

  1. $\text{(i) and (ii)}$
  2. $\text{(i) and (iii)}$
  3. $\text{(ii) and (iii)}$
  4. $\text{(i) and (iv)}$

go:359795.title: GATE ECE 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 6
go:359795.question:
Given below are two statements and two conclusions.

  • $\text{Statement 1}$: All purple are green.
  • $\text{Statement 2}$: All black are green.
  • $\text{Conclusion I}$: Some black are purple.
  • $\text{Conclusion II}$: No black is purple.

Based on the above statements and conclusions, which one of the following options is logically $\text{CORRECT}$?

  1. Only conclusion $\text{I}$ is correct
  2. Only conclusion $\text{II}$ is correct
  3. Either conclusion $\text{I}$ or $\text{II}$ is correct
  4. Both conclusion $\text{I}$ and $\text{II}$ are correct

go:359793.title: GATE ECE 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 7
go:359793.question:
Computers are ubiquitous. They are used to improve efficiency in almost all fields from agriculture to space exploration. Artificial intelligence $\text{(AI)}$ is currently a hot topic. $\text{Al}$ enables computers to learn, given enough training data. For humans, sitting in front of a computer for long hours can lead to health issues.

Which of the following can be deduced from the above passage?

  1. Nowadays, computers are present in almost all places.
  2. Computers cannot be used for solving problems in engineering
  3. For humans, there are both positive and negative effects of using computers.
  4. Artificial intelligence can be done without data.
  1. $\text{(ii) and (iii)}$
  2. $\text{(ii) and (iv)}$
  3. $\text{(i), (iii) and (iv)}$
  4. $\text{(i) and (iii)}$

go:359791.title: GATE ECE 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 8
go:359791.question:
Consider a square sheet of side $1$ unit. In the first step, it is cut along the main diagonal to get two triangles. In the next step. one of the cut triangles is revolved about its short edge to form a solid cone. The volume of the resulting cone, in cubic units, is ____________

  1. $\frac{\pi }{3}$
  2. $\frac{2\pi }{3}$
  3. $\frac{3\pi }{2}$
  4. $3\pi$

go:359785.title: GATE ECE 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 10
go:359785.question:
​​

Corners are cut from an equilateral triangle to produce a regular convex hexagon as shown in the figure above.

The ratio of the area of the regular convex hexagon to the area of the original equilateral triangle is

  1. $2:3$
  2. $3:4$
  3. $4:5$
  4. $5:6$

go:359757.title: GATE Electrical 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 1
go:359757.question:
The people _______________ were at the demonstration were from all sections of society.

  1. whose
  2. which
  3. who
  4. whom

go:359752.title: GATE Electrical 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 3
go:359752.question:
For a regular polygon having $10$ sides, the interior angle between the sides of the polygon, in degrees, is:

  1. $396$
  2. $324$
  3. $216$
  4. $144$

go:359749.title: GATE Electrical 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 4
go:359749.question:
Which one of the following numbers is exactly divisible by $\left ( 11^{13} +1\right )$?

  1. $11^{26} +1$
  2. $11^{33} +1$
  3. $11^{39} -1$
  4. $11^{52} -1$

go:359747.title: GATE Electrical 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 5
go:359747.question:
$\textit{Oasis}$ is to $\textit{sand}$ as $\textit{island}$ is to _____________

Which one of the following options maintains a similar logical relation in the above sentence?

  1. Stone
  2. Land
  3. Water
  4. Mountain

go:359743.title: GATE Electrical 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 7
go:359743.question:
In the figure shown above, each inside square is formed by joining the midpoints of the sides of the next larger square. The area of the smallest square (shaded) as shown, in $\text{cm}^{2}$ is:

  1. $12.50$
  2. $6.25$
  3. $3.125$
  4. $1.5625$

go:359741.title: GATE Electrical 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 8
go:359741.question:
Let $X$ be a continuous random variable denoting the temperature measured. The range of temperature is $[0, 100]$ degree Celsius and let the probability density function of $X$ be $f\left ( x \right )=0.01$ for $0\leq X\leq 100$.

The mean of $X$ is __________

  1. $2.5$
  2. $5.0$
  3. $25.0$
  4. $50.0$

go:359737.title: GATE Electrical 2021 | General Aptitude Question: 10
go:359737.question:
Seven cars $\text{P, Q, R, S, T, U and V}$ are parked in a row not necessarily in that order. The cars $T$ and $U$ should be parked next to each other. The cars $S$ and $V$ also should be parked next to each other, whereas $P$ and $Q$ cannot be parked next to each other. $Q$ and $S$ must be parked next to each other. $R$ is parked to the immediate right of $V$. $T$ is parked to the left of $U$.

Based on the above statements, the only $\text{INCORRECT}$ option given below is:

  1. There are two cars parked in between $Q$ and $V$.
  2. $Q$ and $R$ are not parked together.
  3. $V$ is the only car parked in between $S$ and $R$.
  4. Car $P$ is parked at the extreme end.
